data.keyword: What is community-based mental health care, and who provides it in Louisville?
data.tag: lmhg ai mode expanded What is community-based mental health care, and who provides it in Louisville?

result.keyword: What is community-based mental health care, and who provides it in Louisville?

items.markdown:
Community-based mental health care `brings mental health services directly into the environments where people live, work, and connect` . Instead of relying solely on institutional psychiatric hospitals, it focuses on outpatient treatments, crisis intervention, peer support, and rehabilitation within the local community. This evidence-based model prioritizes accessibility, long-term recovery, and integrated care for vulnerable populations.[](https://www.who.int/news-room/commentaries/detail/from-isolation-to-inclusion---community-based-mental-health-care) [[1]](https://www.who.int/news-room/commentaries/detail/from-isolation-to-inclusion---community-based-mental-health-care)[[2]](https://publichealth.tulane.edu/blog/what-is-community-mental-health/)[[3]](https://findhelpnow.org/ky/facilities/UHVibGljRmFjaWxpdHlOb2RlOjMwNTA=)[[4]](https://www.facebook.com/TonyAmandaMetcalfe/)[[5]](https://csgjusticecenter.org/resources/funding/opportunities/community-mental-health-services-block-grant/)

Several major organizations and clinics provide this care in Louisville:

Key Providers in Louisville

- **[Seven Counties Services](https://sevencounties.org/):** Serving as Kentucky's leading Community Mental Health Center (CMHC), this organization operates several Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic (CCBHC) locations across Jefferson County. They provide comprehensive services for behavioral health and substance use, regardless of insurance status.[](https://sevencounties.org/) [[1]](https://sevencounties.org/)[[2]](https://findhelpnow.org/ky/facilities/UHVibGljRmFjaWxpdHlOb2RlOjMwNTA=)
- **[UofL Health - Peace Hospital](https://uoflhealth.org/services/mental-and-behavioral-health/):** One of the largest private psychiatric facilities in the country, it provides both inpatient stabilization and extensive outpatient community programs, including no-charge assessments.[](https://uoflhealth.org/services/mental-and-behavioral-health/) [[1]](https://uoflhealth.org/services/mental-and-behavioral-health/)[[2]](https://uoflhealth.org/articles/helping-the-public-access-behavioral-health-treatment/)[[3]](https://uoflhealth.org/services/mental-and-behavioral-health/adult-outpatient-services/)[[4]](https://startyourrecovery.org/facility/uofl-health-peace-hospital-louisville-ky)[[5]](https://uoflhealth.org/services/mental-and-behavioral-health/substance-abuse/)
- **[Park DuValle Community Health Center](https://www.pdchc.org/):** A Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC) that provides integrated behavioral health alongside primary care services, located primarily in the West End of Louisville.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://www.mightycause.com/organization/Park-Duvalle-Community-Health-Center)[[3]](https://recruiting.paylocity.com/recruiting/jobs/Details/3654403/Park-DuValle-Community-Health-Centers/Peer-Support-Specialist)[[4]](https://www.courier-journal.com/story/news/local/2025/05/28/park-duvalle-community-health-centers-to-build-community-food-center/83472262007/)
- **Collective Care Center at Spalding University:** Offers affordable therapy and assessment services to the broader Louisville community, provided by graduate-level students under expert clinical supervision.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)
- **Louisville Recovery Community Connection:** Provides community-based peer support, mentorship, and recovery resources for individuals struggling with substance use.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://www.findhelp.org/housing/help-find-housing--lexington-ky)[[3]](https://startyourrecovery.org/treatment/rehab-centers/kentucky/lyndon)

Crisis Resources & Support Hubs

- **[Mental Health Lou Wellness Hub](https://www.mentalhealthlou.com/):** A local initiative aimed at connecting residents to local wellness resources, training, and mental health advocacy groups.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://www.mentalhealthlou.com/about)[[3]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/american-rescue-plan/public-health-contingencies)
- **Seven Counties Crisis Lines:** They operate 24/7 localized support:
	- Adult Crisis Line: **(502) 589-4313** 
	- Child Crisis Line: **(502) 589-8070** [](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://www.giveforgoodlouisville.org/organization/Citizens-Of-Louisville-Organized-And-United-Together)[[3]](https://sevencounties.org/crisis/)
- **988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline:** Simply call or text **988** for immediate, free, and confidential support, 24/7.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://vahemophilia.org/community-resources/mental-health/)[[3]](https://www.mywoc.org/suicide-intervention-prevention-resources/)[[4]](https://www.contactsyracuse.org/contact-community-services-celebrates-two-year-anniversary-of-988-suicide-crisis-lifeline/)[[5]](https://www.facebook.com/msudenverhealthinstitute/)

Would you like to explore **insurance-based options**, **specialized trauma therapy** , or **substance use recovery programs** available in the Louisville area?

Instead of relying solely on institutional psychiatric hospitals, it focuses on outpatient treatments, crisis intervention, peer support, and rehabilitation within the local community. This evidence-based model prioritizes accessibility, long-term recovery, and integrated care for vulnerable populations. World Health Organization (WHO) +1

They provide comprehensive services for behavioral health and substance use, regardless of insurance status.
